title: PPS School ERP — Full Admin & Teacher Portal (Frontend Implementation)

requirements.summary: Implement login screen with role selector, username, password, school code fields, and credential validation routing users to their respective dashboards
acceptanceCriteria: Login screen shows role selector, username, password, and school code fields
Wrong school code shows an error and blocks login
Admin logs in with correct credentials and lands on Admin dashboard
Teacher logs in with admin-assigned credentials and lands on Teacher dashboard
Student logs in with admin-assigned credentials and lands on Student dashboard

file_operations.description: Create login page with role selector (Admin/Teacher/Student), username field, password field, school code field. Validate school code against 'palakpublicschool'. Use the authorization component to authenticate users and retrieve their role. On successful login, route to the appropriate dashboard based on role. Display error messages for invalid credentials or school code. Wire the generated PPS logo from frontend/public/assets/generated/pps-logo.dim_256x256.png into the login screen header.

Add routing for /login, /admin-dashboard, /teacher-dashboard. Set up protected routes that check authentication status using the authorization component. Redirect unauthenticated users to /login. Default route should redirect to login if not authenticated.

Create authentication hook that wraps the authorization component's useInternetIdentity hook. Provide login, logout, and role-checking utilities. Store and retrieve UserProfile from backend using getCallerUserProfile and saveCallerUserProfile. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Build Admin dashboard with bottom navigation containing Home, Session, Students, Teachers, Teacher Timetable, Teacher Academics, Lesson Plan, Event Calendar, Greetings, Leave, and top tab bar for sub-modules
acceptanceCriteria: Admin bottom navigation bar has all specified items
Tapping a bottom nav item loads the correct module
Inside Session > Class, a top tab bar shows Class Students, Class Timetable, Class Subject, Class Teacher, Fees

file_operations.description: Create admin layout with bottom navigation bar containing: Home (Circulars), Session, Students, Teachers, Teacher Timetable, Teacher Academics, Lesson Plan, Event Calendar, Greetings, Leave. Use orange accent color for active navigation state. Apply mobile-first responsive design. Include page outlet for module content.
Create admin dashboard wrapper that uses AdminLayout. Set up routing for all admin modules accessible from bottom navigation.

Create reusable top tab bar component for sub-module navigation (e.g., Class Students, Class Timetable, Class Subject, Class Teacher, Fees). Support horizontal scrolling for multiple tabs. Use orange accent for active tab.
Wire admin dashboard and all admin sub-routes into the main router under /admin-dashboard. Ensure protected route guards using authorization component.

requirements.summary: Implement Circulars module for Admin with list view, full view, create form with multi-image attachments, and edit/delete actions
acceptanceCriteria: Circular list shows title and 2-line preview
Full circular view shows date bold centered at top, title, description, attachments in black
New circular form has title, description, session select, class multi-select (select all / specific), optional multiple attachments
Three-dots menu on each circular has Edit and Delete
Edit pre-fills existing data; Delete removes the circular

file_operations.description: Create circulars page showing list of circulars with title and first two lines of description. Include '+' button to open create form. Each circular row has three-dots menu with Edit and Delete. Use backend methods: getAllCirculars, createCircular, updateCircular, deleteCircular. Use the blob-storage component to handle image attachments. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create full circular view showing date bold and centered at top, title, description in black text on white background, and attached images. Use ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() for image rendering.

Create circular form component with fields: title, description, session dropdown (from getAllSessions), class multi-select (with 'Select All' option), and multiple image attachments (using blob-storage component's ExternalBlob.fromBytes with upload progress tracking). Form should support both create and edit modes, pre-filling data in edit mode.
Add routes for /admin-dashboard/circulars and /admin-dashboard/circulars/:id under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Sessions module for Admin with session creation, class addition, and top tab navigation for class sub-modules
acceptanceCriteria: Sessions are created by name and listed in history
Classes are added within a session and listed under it
Tapping a class opens top tabs: Class Students, Class Timetable, Class Subject, Class Teacher, Fees
Session visibility settings: admin can separately control which sessions are visible to students and to teachers

file_operations.description: Create sessions page with '+' button to add new session by name. Display session history list. Use backend methods: createSession, getAllSessions. Each session row is tappable to view its classes.

Create session detail page showing '+' button to add classes by name. Display list of classes under the session. Use backend method: addClassToSession. Each class row is tappable to open class detail with top tabs.
Create class detail page with TopTabBar component showing tabs: Class Students, Class Timetable, Class Subject, Class Teacher, Fees. Render appropriate content based on active tab.
Add routes for /admin-dashboard/sessions, /admin-dashboard/sessions/:sessionId, /admin-dashboard/sessions/:sessionId/classes/:classId under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Class Students under Session > Class with add, edit, view, pass (transfer), and delete student operations
acceptanceCriteria: Add Student form captures all required and optional fields
Student list is alphabetical by first name with adm. no
Edit pre-fills all fields; View shows read-only profile
Pass opens session then class selector; student transfers with credentials intact
Delete removes student from all views

file_operations.description: Create class students component with 'Add Student' button. Display alphabetical student list by first name with admission number. Each student has three-dots menu with Edit, View, Pass, Delete. Use backend methods: addStudent, getStudentsByClass, updateStudent, deleteStudent. Pass action should allow session and class selection, preserving student credentials.

Create student form with fields: name, admission no, Aadhaar no, date of birth, date of joining, address, class (auto-set), father name, mother name, father mobile, mother mobile, father/mother occupation, guardian info (optional), username, password, code. Support create and edit modes with pre-filling in edit mode.
Create read-only student profile view component displaying all student information.

requirements.summary: Implement Class Timetable under Session > Class with period entry form, tabular display, and day filtering
acceptanceCriteria: Period form has period name (optional), period no, day selector
Blank period name saves as 'Free Period' shown in green
Timetable displayed in tabular form with bold large period names
Day filter at top of history filters visible periods
Final Save button persists the full timetable

file_operations.description: Create class timetable component with 'Create New Timetable' button. Form to add periods with: period name (optional, blank becomes 'Free Period' in green), period number, day dropdown (Monday–Saturday). '+' button adds next period. 'Save' button at bottom persists timetable. Display history with day filter dropdown at top. Use backend methods: createTimetable, getTimetable. Show period names in bold large text in tabular view.

requirements.summary: Implement Class Subject under Session > Class with subject creation, teacher assignment, and alphabetical display
acceptanceCriteria: Subject form has subject name field and teacher dropdown
Subjects saved alphabetically in tabular form; name bold
All subject pickers throughout the app for a given class use this list as source

file_operations.description: Create class subjects component with 'Create Subject' button. Form with subject name field and teacher selector dropdown (from getAllTeachers filtered by session). Display subjects alphabetically in tabular form with subject name bold and teacher name below. Store subject list in context or state for use by subject pickers throughout the app.
Create hook to fetch and provide class-specific subject list for use in subject dropdowns throughout the app. Ensure no free-text subject entry is allowed.

requirements.summary: Implement Fees module under Session > Class with Total, Approved, Pending tabs, monthly entry, and automatic late fee accrual
acceptanceCriteria: Three tabs: Total, Approved, Pending
Total tab lists students; per student shows 12-month table
Per month entry captures Approved, Pending, Total amounts
Update button in history allows re-editing fee entry
Approved and Pending tabs require month selection then show filtered alphabetical student table
Late fee of ₹30 accrues every 5 days when pending > ₹2000

file_operations.description: Create class fees component with top tabs: Total, Approved, Pending. Total tab: alphabetical student list; tap student shows 12-month table (January–December); tap month shows fields for Approved Amount, Pending Amount, Total Amount with Save button and Update button in history. Approved and Pending tabs: month selector first, then alphabetical student table with name, admission no, and relevant amount. Display late fee accrual notice (₹30 per 5 days when pending > ₹2000). Backend handles late fee calculation; frontend displays it.

requirements.summary: Implement top-level Students module for Admin with session selector, add student, and class-wise listing with edit/view/pass/delete
acceptanceCriteria: Session selector shown; students listed class-wise alphabetically
Add Student opens full form
Three-dots: Edit, View, Pass, Delete all functional

file_operations.description: Create top-level students page with session selector at top and 'Add Student' button at top center. Display class-wise alphabetical list of students with name and admission number. Each student has three-dots menu with Edit, View, Pass, Delete. Reuse StudentForm and StudentProfile components from REQ-5. Use backend methods: addStudent, getStudentsByClass, updateStudent, deleteStudent.
Add route for /admin-dashboard/students under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement top-level Teachers module for Admin with session selector, add teacher form, and teacher listing with view/edit/delete
acceptanceCriteria: Add Teacher form captures all specified fields
Teacher list sorted by teacher number
Three-dots: View, Edit, Delete functional
Delete removes teacher from all views

file_operations.description: Create teachers page with session selector and 'Add New Teacher' button. Display teacher list sorted by teacher number with name. Each teacher has three-dots menu with View, Edit, Delete. Use backend methods: addTeacher, getAllTeachers, updateTeacher, deleteTeacher.

Create teacher form with fields: teacher name, date of birth, joining date, qualification, father name, mother name, contact number, teacher no, email, address, username, password, code. Support create and edit modes with pre-filling in edit mode.
Create read-only teacher profile view component displaying all teacher information.
Add route for /admin-dashboard/teachers under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement top-level Teacher Timetable module for Admin with teacher selection, period entry, day-based viewing, and edit/delete
acceptanceCriteria: Add timetable form has teacher, day, period no, period name (optional), class (optional)
Blank period name shown as 'Free Period' in green
History lists by teacher name; tap → day select → tabular timetable
Three-dots: Edit and Delete

file_operations.description: Create teacher timetable page with session selector and 'Add New Timetable' button. Form: teacher dropdown, day (Monday–Saturday), period no, period name (optional = Free Period in green), class selection (optional, blank = free period). 'Next' adds another period; 'Done & Save' persists. History lists by teacher name; tap teacher → select day → view tabular timetable (period no, period name bold, class, subject). Three-dots per entry: Edit, Delete.
Add route for /admin-dashboard/teacher-timetable under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement top-level Greetings module for Admin with multi-image greeting creation, session/class targeting, and edit/delete
acceptanceCriteria: Add greeting form has title, description, session, class selector, multi-image (JPG) upload
History shows title with three-dots (Edit / Delete)
Edit pre-fills all fields

file_operations.description: Create greetings page with '+' button to add greeting. Form: title, description, session selection, class selection (all or specific), multiple JPG image attachments (using blob-storage component's ExternalBlob). History shows title with three-dots menu (Edit, Delete). Edit pre-fills all fields including images. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add route for /admin-dashboard/greetings under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement top-level Lesson Plan module for Admin to view teacher-submitted lesson plans with full details
acceptanceCriteria: Lesson plan list shows teacher name
Tapping shows full lesson plan details
Admin can save lesson plans to history

file_operations.description: Create lesson plan page showing list of lesson plans submitted by teachers. Display teacher name per entry. Tap to open full details: session, class, subject, topic, start date, end date. Admin can save to history.
Add route for /admin-dashboard/lesson-plan under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement top-level Event Calendar module for Admin with session-based monthly calendar, event creation, and read-only teacher/student viewing
acceptanceCriteria: Session selector then calendar view
Tapping date opens description field and saves on confirm
Events shown on calendar dates
Teachers and students can view events (read-only)

file_operations.description: Create event calendar page with session selector. Display monthly calendar view. Tapping a date opens description input field; saving stores event for that date. Saved events visible on calendar dates.
Add route for /admin-dashboard/event-calendar under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement top-level Academics module for Admin with sub-options: Progress, Leave, Subject, Teacher Academics, Salary, Attendance
acceptanceCriteria: Academics section is accessible from admin navigation
Sub-options: Progress, Leave, Subject, Teacher Academics, Salary, Attendance are all accessible

file_operations.description: Create academics page with navigation to sub-modules: Progress, Leave, Subject, Teacher Academics, Salary, Attendance. Use vertical menu or card-based navigation.
Add route for /admin-dashboard/academics under admin protected routes with nested routes for each sub-module.

requirements.summary: Implement Progress under Admin > Academics with progress batch creation, subject-wise mark entry, teacher dispatch, and approval workflow
acceptanceCriteria: Progress form: title, total marks, student multi-select, subject select
Done & Send dispatches to subject teacher
Teacher can enter obtained marks and submit back
Completed subjects show √ in admin history
Approve sends finalized results to students
Subject order preserved throughout

file_operations.description: Create progress page with session selector → class selector. Add title for progress batch. 'New Progress' entry form: total marks, student selection (all or specific), subject selection (from class subjects). 'Save and Next' adds more subjects. 'Done & Send' dispatches to subject teacher. History shows subjects with checkmark (√) for completed; 'Approve' button sends results to students. Preserve subject order as added.
Add route for /admin-dashboard/academics/progress under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Leave under Admin > Academics showing teacher leave requests with approval/rejection workflow
acceptanceCriteria: Teacher list shown after session select; absent/leave teachers highlighted
Teacher leave detail view shows all specified fields
Approve/Reject buttons notify the teacher

file_operations.description: Create leave page with session selector. Display list of teachers in session; highlight teachers on leave. Tap teacher to show: teacher name, teacher no, class incharge (or 'No Class Teacher'), leave days, leave date, leave reason. Approve and Reject buttons send decision back to teacher.
Add route for /admin-dashboard/academics/leave under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Subject under Admin > Academics for class-specific subject management ensuring all subject pickers use this list
acceptanceCriteria: Session > class > add subject by name
Subject saved to history for that class
Subject pickers in all modules for a class use this list

file_operations.description: Create subject management page with session selector → class selector. 'Add Subject' button with subject name field. Display saved subjects in history for that class. Integrate with useClassSubjects hook to ensure all subject pickers throughout the app use this list.
Add route for /admin-dashboard/academics/subject under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Teacher Academics under Admin > Academics with per-teacher tabs for Class Incharge, Timetable, Classes & Subjects, Attendance, Salary, Teacher Detail
acceptanceCriteria: Teacher list sorted by teacher no
Top tab bar: Class Incharge, Timetable, Classes & Subjects, Attendance, Salary, Teacher Detail
Class Incharge saved/edited/deleted
Attendance calendar with all 5 status options; no mark = present
Salary auto-calculates based on attendance; monthly history
Classes & Subjects tabular history

file_operations.description: Create teacher academics page with session selector. Display teacher list sorted by teacher number. Tapping teacher opens detail page with top tab bar: Class Incharge, Timetable, Classes & Subjects, Attendance, Salary, Teacher Detail.

Create teacher academics detail component with TopTabBar for: (1) Class Incharge: select class or 'No Class Teacher', history with three-dots (Edit, Delete). (2) Timetable: tabular view. (3) Classes & Subjects: 'Add New' → class → subject; tabular history. (4) Attendance: 'Add Attendance' → calendar → mark present/absent/half-day/leave/holiday (no mark = present); monthly history with three-dots (Edit). (5) Salary: per-day amount (admin sets), total amount, deducted amount (auto), absent days (auto), holiday days (auto), total salary (auto = present_days × per_day_amount); monthly history with three-dots. (6) Teacher Detail: read-only profile.
Add routes for /admin-dashboard/academics/teacher-academics and /admin-dashboard/academics/teacher-academics/:teacherId under admin protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Session Visibility Settings under Admin > Session to control which sessions are visible to students and teachers
acceptanceCriteria: Visible Sessions has Student and Teacher sub-sections
Admin can toggle session visibility per role
Student entry session is separately configurable

file_operations.description: Create session visibility settings component accessible from session detail page. Two sub-sections: Students and Teachers. Admin can toggle session visibility per role using checkboxes. Separate 'Student Entry Session' dropdown to control which session new students are entered into.
Add 'Visible Sessions' button/link in session detail page that opens SessionVisibilitySettings component in a modal or dedicated view.

requirements.summary: Build Teacher dashboard with session selector, bottom navigation (Home, Communication, Profile, Menu), and module access based on admin-allowed sessions
acceptanceCriteria: Teacher bottom nav: Home, Communication, Profile, Menu
Home shows today's circulars for teacher's classes
Full circular view in black with title, description, attachments
Communication: class select, student search, chat with text + multi-type attachments
Profile shows read-only teacher info
Menu lists all 10 sub-items

file_operations.description: Create teacher layout with session selector (from admin-allowed sessions only) and bottom navigation bar: Home, Communication, Profile, Menu. Use orange accent for active navigation. Apply mobile-first responsive design.
Create teacher dashboard wrapper using TeacherLayout. Set up routing for teacher modules.

Create teacher home page showing circulars for today sent to teacher's classes. Display date/month/day bold at top center. List circulars with title and preview; tap to view full circular in black text (title, description, attachments) on white background using CircularDetail component.

Create communication page with pre-selected session, class selector, and student search. Tap student to open chat interface with text input and '+' button for attachments (image/audio/video/file using blob-storage component). Send button saves to history. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create teacher profile page displaying read-only teacher information using TeacherProfile component.
Create teacher menu page with navigation to 10 sub-items: My Class, My Timetable, Leave, Salary, Progress, Homework, Lesson Plan, Event Calendar, Greetings, My Academics.
Add routes for /teacher-dashboard and all teacher sub-routes under teacher protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Teacher > Menu > My Class with top tabs for Class Incharge, Class Students, Class Timetable, Attendance, Leave, Subjects (visible only to class teachers)
acceptanceCriteria: Top tab bar with 6 items visible only to class teachers
Class Students: alphabetical list; tap shows full profile
Class Timetable: day select then tabular view
Attendance: per-student calendar marking; monthly history
Leave: per-student approval/rejection
Subjects: alphabetical with teacher names

file_operations.description: Create my class page with guard: visible only to class teachers (assigned as class incharge). Top tab bar: Class Incharge, Class Students, Class Timetable, Attendance, Leave, Subjects. (1) Class Incharge: shows assigned class or 'No Class Incharge'. (2) Class Students: alphabetical list; tap shows full profile. (3) Class Timetable: day selector → tabular timetable. (4) Attendance: student list → tap student → calendar → mark absent/half-day/leave/holiday (no mark = present); monthly history with three-dots (Edit, Delete). (5) Leave: tap student → enter date, days, reason → Approve/Reject. (6) Subjects: alphabetical list with teacher names.
Add route for /teacher-dashboard/my-class under teacher protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Teacher > Menu > My Timetable with day selector and tabular timetable display
acceptanceCriteria: Day selector at top
Timetable shown in tabular form with period no, period name bold, class, subject

file_operations.description: Create my timetable page with day selector dropdown at top. Display teacher's timetable in tabular form: period no, period name (bold), class, subject.
Add route for /teacher-dashboard/my-timetable under teacher protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Teacher > Menu > Leave with My Leave and Other Leave tabs for leave submission and viewing
acceptanceCriteria: Two tabs: My Leave and Other Leave
Add Leave form: day, date, reason, class selector
Leave result (Approved/Rejected) shown in history
Other Leave tab shows all teachers' leaves

file_operations.description: Create teacher leave page with two tabs: My Leave and Other Leave. (1) My Leave: 'Add Leave' form with day, date, reason, class incharge select (class or 'No Class Teacher'); submit sends to admin; history shows result (Approved/Rejected). (2) Other Leave: read-only list of all teachers' leave records.
Add route for /teacher-dashboard/leave under teacher protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Teacher > Menu > Salary with month selector displaying total, deducted, and approved amounts
acceptanceCriteria: Month selector at top
Shows Total, Deducted, Approved amounts for that month

file_operations.description: Create teacher salary page with month selector dropdown. Display: Total Amount, Deducted Amount, Approved Amount for selected month (sourced from backend salary data and attendance records).
Add route for /teacher-dashboard/salary under teacher protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Teacher > Menu > Progress for entering obtained marks per student and submitting to admin
acceptanceCriteria: Assigned classes listed
Progress title and student list visible
Teacher can enter obtained marks per student and submit to admin

file_operations.description: Create teacher progress page showing classes assigned by admin. Tap class → show progress titles → tap title → alphabetical student list with teacher's subject → enter obtained marks per student → save and send back to admin.
Add route for /teacher-dashboard/progress under teacher protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Teacher > Menu > Homework with form for session, class, title, description, and multi-attachments
acceptanceCriteria: Homework form: session, class, title, description, multi-attachment (image/video)
Homework saved to history

file_operations.description: Create homework page with 'Add Homework' form: session select, class select, title, description, attachments (images and videos, multiple allowed using blob-storage component). Save to history. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add route for /teacher-dashboard/homework under teacher protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Teacher > Menu > Lesson Plan with form for session, class, subject, topic, dates, and submission to admin
acceptanceCriteria: Lesson plan form: session, class, subject (dropdown), topic, start date, end date
Send & Save stores and dispatches to admin

file_operations.description: Create teacher lesson plan page with form: session select, class select, subject select (from class subjects using useClassSubjects hook), topic, start date, end date. 'Send & Save' button sends to admin and saves to teacher history.
Add route for /teacher-dashboard/lesson-plan under teacher protected routes.

requirements.summary: Implement Teacher > Menu > My Academics with top tabs for Class Incharge, Classes to Teach, My Attendance
acceptanceCriteria: Top tab bar: Class Incharge, Classes to Teach, My Attendance
Classes to Teach: tabular with class and subject columns
My Attendance: read-only personal attendance history

file_operations.description: Create my academics page with top tab bar: Class Incharge, Classes to Teach, My Attendance. (1) Class Incharge: shows class teacher is incharge of. (2) Classes to Teach: tabular list with class and subject columns. (3) My Attendance: read-only view of own attendance records.
Add route for /teacher-dashboard/my-academics under teacher protected routes.

requirements.summary: Apply consistent orange color theme throughout the app with clean typography, mobile-first layout, and black text for circular content
acceptanceCriteria: Primary color throughout UI is orange (e.g., #F97316 or similar)
Circular full-view text rendered in black
Bottom navigation bar uses orange for active state
Mobile-first responsive layout
Consistent typography hierarchy across all screens

file_operations.description: Define orange color theme as primary throughout the app (e.g., #F97316 or Tailwind's orange-500). Apply to action buttons, active navigation states, and key UI elements. Ensure circular full-view text uses black color on white/light background. Set up clean typography hierarchy with readable fonts. Establish mobile-first responsive utilities.

Configure orange as the primary color in Tailwind theme extend. Set up custom color palette with orange variants for hover, active, and disabled states. Ensure consistent spacing, typography, and responsive breakpoints for mobile-first design.
